summary: Project direction is at an architectural fork: keep ICP-native KD Platform (React + Motoko) OR build a standalone Web2 stack (“KD Enterprise”) with Node.js/Express + MySQL + JWT + static HTML/JS frontend (and optionally a Netlify-hosted “KA.ai + KD Enterprise” identity-gated dashboard with serverless functions for AI proxy, token generation, and analytics).
architectural_notes: Project stack includes: static frontend (HTML/CSS/JS) + Node.js/Express API + MySQL DB; frontend fetches backend API at http://localhost:5000/api.
Backend should use environment variables for PORT and DB credentials; include basic security headers middleware.
Need explicit decision on runtime target: ICP-native (React+Motoko) vs standalone Web2 (Node/Express/MySQL); the Node/MySQL stack cannot run inside the ICP draft environment.
If pursuing KA.ai + KD Enterprise on Netlify: use Netlify Identity + Functions for AI proxy/token/analytics; frontend is static with serverless backend endpoints.
known_issues: Draft app environment may expire and require rebuild/redeploy; treat as tooling hiccup rather than design issue.
Attempting to generate an entire full-stack codebase in a single build/prompt caused builder confusion/failure; refinement should be done in smaller, staged layers.

existing_features.title: Add a new KD Platform landing page in the existing React app that mirrors the user-provided `index.html` intent (headline + CTA button), but implemented as a React route and styled with Tailwind.
Add a new KD Dashboard page route that mirrors the user-provided `dashboard.html` intent, including a ‘Total Users’ card that fetches and displays a live user count from the Motoko canister.
Add a ‘KD Platform’ entry in the existing site navigation (desktop + mobile) that links to the new KD Platform landing page route.
Show a token contract placeholder section on the KD dashboard page using the exact placeholder text provided by the user.

Apply a coherent KD Platform visual theme distinct from the existing maritime theme, inspired by the user’s provided dark + neon-teal styling, and use it consistently across the new KD landing + dashboard pages.

Add a minimal admin-only user management workflow that replaces the user’s requested Web2 register/login approach: expose an admin-only canister method to add users (name + email) and a frontend form on the KD dashboard to call it.
Add a KD Platform dashboard guard that prompts the user to authenticate with Internet Identity before loading protected KD dashboard data, using clear English user-facing messaging.

new_feature_requests.summary: Create KD Platform full project scaffold: frontend (HTML/CSS/JS) with index + dashboard pages and basic navigation
Implement Node.js/Express backend API with CORS + JSON parsing and basic routes (e.g., /api/users count)
Add MySQL database setup and integration (db connection, schema for users, seed admin user)
Add environment configuration (.env) and deployment-ready project structure/README run instructions
Add basic production security headers middleware in backend
Add token placeholder display/integration point in dashboard UI
Implement a Motoko backend API equivalent to `GET /api/users` that returns the total user count from canister state, and expose it through the generated frontend declarations for React Query to call.

Add minimal user records support in the Motoko canister to replace the requested MySQL `users` table, including an admin-only method to add users and a safe default/seed so the dashboard can display a non-zero count in a fresh deployment.
Build standalone KD Enterprise Web2 stack: Node.js/Express REST API + MySQL + JWT auth + security headers + static HTML/CSS/JS pages (index/login/register/dashboard)
Add KA.ai + KD Enterprise Netlify deployment package: Netlify Identity gate + serverless functions (Perplexity proxy, KD token generator, analytics) + A2 SaaS dark UI

Ensure the KD Platform dashboard user count works on a fresh deployment by seeding at least one user record in the Motoko canister when the platform is first initialized (so the dashboard does not show 0 unless the admin explicitly clears users).
